chunks.text:
Initial Prompt

The initial prompt for the Resume Customizer integrated my resume details and a job description for a data analyst contract position. A job description from WhatJobs was utilized as the “job description” for this resume example. This output was generated by entering my resume information (available at https://chris.com.ph) into the Resume Customizer.



Job Description

Position: Data Analyst

Schedule: Onsite 5 days/week 8-5 M-F

Duration: 6 Month Contract

Pay Rate: $25-$30/hr



Job Description:

A large retail/e-commerce client is looking to bring on a temporary resource to support a team with Customs and Trade Compliance activities. This role will primarily focus on data intensive work and analytical support as we manage ongoing compliance requirements.



The ideal candidate does not need prior customs or trade compliance experience. However, strong data management skills are critical, particularly advanced or highly proficient in Excel capabilities. This individu

iance experience. However, strong data management skills are critical, particularly advanced or highly proficient in Excel capabilities. This individual should be comfortable working with large data sets, adapting quickly to shifting facts or priorities, and maintaining accuracy under changing conditions.



Beyond technical skills, the most important competency is mindset. Were looking for someone who is naturally curious, enjoys problem?solving, and is willing to dig into complex issues to find practical solutions. In terms of role alignment, this position is most comparable to a Customs Specialist, with an emphasis on analytical and operational support rather than regulatory expertise.



Initial Output



CHRISTOPHER FORNESA

Houston, TX

christopher.fornesa@email.com | (XXX) XXX-XXXX | linkedin.com/in/christopherfornesa



SUMMARY

Creative data and analytics professional with five years of experience turning complex data into actionable insights. Skilled in quantitative analysis

reative data and analytics professional with five years of experience turning complex data into actionable insights. Skilled in quantitative analysis and communication to uncover policy insights and spread public awareness. Committed to ethical, data-driven work that serves communities and transforms the world. Adept at managing large datasets, adapting to shifting priorities, and maintaining accuracy under changing conditions. Naturally curious problem-solver with a mindset for digging into complex issues to find practical solutions.



SKILLS

- Quantitative Analysis

- Research Design

- Data-Driven Decision Making

- Program Evaluation

- Report Writing

- Data Storytelling

- Advanced Excel

- Power BI

- SharePoint



EXPERIENCE

MOBILE DATA SME

Chevron · Houston, TX

May 2024 - July 2025

Prompt Chaining

After reading the initial output, I used the following prompt to make specific changes that I believed were necessary.

Second Prompt

‘Please make the summary more concise, limit the skills section to 5 skills, and only retain the most relevant 3 awards, changing the "Awards" header to "Certificates".’

To ensure accuracy and that my “true” voice shines through in the resume, I made manual corrections to include details, using the template provided in the second output.

een 2024 and 2025 to increase transparency in enterprise mobility, enabling 60% faster decisions and at least 20% cost savings.

Agentic Assistance

Using my manually “humanized” resume, I prompted the agent to make even more specific changes. First, I contextualized my changes, such as mentioning my combination of three different roles, and structural changes in the document. Then I instructed the agent to implement tasks such as providing additional skills and certificates.



Third Prompt

“After further review, I dec

nt. Then I instructed the agent to implement tasks such as providing additional skills and certificates.



Third Prompt

“After further review, I decided to combine the three different roles and change the structure for some of the other sections. However, it now looks like there is some more space to add a few more details, specifically more skills and certificates, while there is some room to make bullet points for the single experience highlighted (the IT analyst role) more concise. Can you assist me with this task? The following contain my new resume details: <resume from the previous step>.”

Third Output

Final Human Corrections

I drafted a final resume to mitigate hallucinations and formatting issues in the previous output. For instance, my previous formatting changes were removed and replaced with the agent’s formatting, while certificates that I never owned, such as the “Certified Analytics Professional Preparation” certificate, were added to my “Certificates” section. This was likely caused by the fact that I did not specifically include prompts to prevent hallucinations, while it revealed that mitigating hallucinations was a necessary feature for the agentic system. I also used the generated diction and structure to add details I had not previously thought to include. I also reformatted the resume contents for readability.

Final Resume

CHRISTOPHER FORNESA

Houston, TX | cfornesa@outlook.com | (281) 400-1920 | li

. I also reformatted the resume contents for readability.
